title: World-first: Life Biosciences dosed first patient with gene therapy to make cells young again
description: Nature reports Life Biosciences treated its first human participant on June 9 with a partial-reprogramming gene therapy activating three Yamanaka-style factors, targeting glaucoma as the inaugural indication. This is the first time a cellular age-reversal therapy has ever been administered to a human and the most significant translational milestone in geroscience to date.

title: Rejuvenate Bio closes $6M financing and strategic R&D deal with Merck Animal Health
description: Rejuvenate Bio raised $6M led by VCapital with Merck Animal Health, Kendall Capital Partners, Connecticut Innovations and Digitalis participating, alongside a strategic R&D collaboration to advance a novel gene therapy program. Merck's involvement signals that big-pharma is now actively partnering on gene-therapy longevity pipelines in companion animals as a regulatory and revenue stepping stone to humans.

title: Contrary Research publishes 'Anti-Aging Therapeutics Boom' market thesis
description: Contrary Research published a comprehensive market memo framing the current wave of partial-reprogramming, senolytics, GLP-1 aging trials, AI-enabled drug discovery and consumer longevity wearables as an investable thematic boom. The piece is being widely circulated on X by longevity investors and is the clearest articulation yet of the venture playbook now forming around the field.

title: 60 Minutes profiles Dog Aging Project rapamycin RCT — NIH-funded national trial advances
description: CBS News' 60 Minutes profiled the NIH-funded Dog Aging Project's large-scale rapamycin RCT, with broader study enrollment exceeding 50,000 dogs and hundreds actively dosed in the rapamycin vs placebo arm. The mainstream coverage is the highest-profile geroscience translation moment in years and a leading indicator of upcoming regulatory dialogue around aging-as-indication.

title: PEARL rapamycin long-term safety trial published — first 48-week human evidence
description: The PEARL trial (115 adults aged 50-85, 48 weeks of 10 mg/week rapamycin) reported preserved muscle mass in women and bone mineral content in men with no serious adverse events. Published over the weekend, it is the strongest long-term human safety and efficacy dataset for the field's gold-standard geroprotector.

title: World-first cellular age-reversal gene therapy given to a human — Nature exclusive
description: Life Biosciences (Boston) announced June 9 that it has treated its first participant in a clinical trial of a partial-reprogramming gene therapy that activates three genes to make aged cells behave young, with glaucoma as the inaugural indication targeting optic nerve neuron regeneration.

title: Rejuvenate Bio announces $6M financing and strategic R&D collaboration with Merck Animal Health
description: $6M round led by VCapital with Merck Animal Health, Kendall Capital Partners, Connecticut Innovations and Digitalis funding continued development of Rejuvenate Bio's gene-therapy platform for age-related and chronic diseases.

title: Contrary Research: The Anti-Aging Therapeutics Boom
description: Contrary Research's investor-facing market memo frames partial reprogramming, senolytics, GLP-1 aging trials, AI-enabled drug discovery and consumer wearables as a structural investable boom — widely circulated by longevity VCs and operators on X.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial could unlock secrets for people
description: 60 Minutes profile of the NIH-funded Dog Aging Project rapamycin RCT — hundreds of dogs dosed on rapamycin vs placebo, with the goal of confirming healthspan extension translates to mammals living human-aligned environments.

title: PEARL trial: rapamycin slows muscle and bone aging in first long-term human RCT
description: Participatory Evaluation of Aging with Rapamycin for Longevity (PEARL) trial in 115 adults 50-85 finds 10 mg/week rapamycin for 48 weeks prevented muscle loss in women and bone loss in men with no serious adverse events.

title: Stanfield and Kaeberlein's new rapamycin trial measures strength and endurance
description: New BMC-registered double-blind RCT testing 6 mg/week sirolimus vs placebo in 40 adults aged 65-80 for 13 weeks, with strength and endurance as primary endpoints.

title: Nature 2026 master review: therapeutic strategies for aging and age-associated diseases
description: Sweeping 2026 review integrating senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, metabolic/dietary interventions, biomarkers, multiomics and AI into a unified translational geroscience framework.

title: UTMB Pepper Center lecture: skeletal muscle as key driver of healthy aging
description: Shalender Bhasin (Harvard Medical School) lecture emphasizes skeletal muscle as a central healthy-aging target with emerging therapies improving strength, function, and healthspan in older adults; recording now available.

title: Longevity Technology Week 23: Ōura Ring 5 launches with BP + GLP-1 insights; Elysium opens TELI longevity clinic
description: Ōura Ring 5 is 40% smaller with week-long battery life, adding blood-pressure signals, breathing monitoring, and GLP-1 insights; Elysium Health opens The Elysium Longevity Institute combining physician oversight with testing, supplements, prescriptions, and peptides.

title: Sinclair receives Honorary Doctorate at UNIC; Life Biosciences gene therapy now in-human
description: David Sinclair conferred Doctor of Philosophy Honoris Causa at UNIC Athens Evolve Lecture; UNIC confirms Life Biosciences' partial-reprogramming gene therapy has reached first-in-human trials targeting glaucoma and NAION.

title: Sinclair + Tally Health publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin and emerging clinical therapies as interventions associated with favorable next-gen epigenetic clock changes (41 human studies).

title: UCSD: Popular GLP-1 drug may slow biological aging — Nature Communications RCT
description: First randomized placebo-controlled clinical evidence that semaglutide slows accumulation of DNA-level biological-aging markers; DunedinPACE -9%, PCGrimAge mortality clock affected over 32 weeks in HIV lipohypertrophy patients.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation
description: Founders Fund-led round (Thrive, Lilly Ventures, Friedman, Gross) brings the Brian Armstrong-founded cell-reprogramming company to $3.1B valuation ahead of its first liver age-reversal human trial.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation launches Evipedia.ai — open evidence encyclopedia for 500+ interventions
description: Michael Greve's Forever Healthy Foundation releases Evipedia.ai covering in-depth evidence reviews of 500+ health and longevity interventions including first-generation rejuvenation therapies, supplements, botanicals, and lifestyle protocols.

title: Cross-species transcriptomic aging clock published in Nature
description: Universal aging signature across mice, monkeys and humans (>11,000 gene activity samples) identifies CDKN1A and LGALS3 as markers tracking mortality and multiple diseases in UK Biobank data.

title: Loss of kidney function correlates with progression of frailty
description: Large cohort analysis: middle-aged and older adults with lower kidney function had higher baseline frailty and faster frailty progression over time.

title: Sirolimus available at TrumpRx at $0.66/mg
description: Generic sirolimus listed on TrumpRx.gov at $0.66/mg — material pricing development for off-label longevity users on weekly rapamycin protocols.

title: Antidepressant mianserin extends mouse lifespan 17.5%
description: Tetracyclic antidepressant mianserin extends median lifespan in mice by 17.5%, with researchers pointing to calcium homeostasis as a potential underlying aging mechanism.

title: Butyrate: microbiome's anti-aging 'kill switch' for senescent cells
description: New discussion of butyrate as a senolytic-like microbiome-derived metabolite that selectively clears senescent cells — implications for fiber/SCFA-focused longevity diets.

title: D+Q senolytic causes brain myelin damage in mice — UConn safety warning
description: UConn researchers show D+Q senolytic at standard longevity doses triggers significant demyelination in corpus callosum white matter in young and old mice, raising urgent concerns about off-label human use.

title: Milken Institute Health June 2026 update — Future of Aging insights
description: Diane Ty (Future of Aging) highlights upcoming Alzheimer's vaccine early-stage trials and the role of biomarkers in enabling earlier diagnosis that pairs with disease-modifying therapies.

title: Peter Attia: The beginning of the end of atherosclerosis?
description: Attia outlines current tools (exercise, sleep, diet, statin-era meds) and emerging technologies that could move atherosclerosis from dominant cause of death to tractable condition.

title: Bryan Johnson publishes 40-point longevity learnings list
description: Johnson distills years of self-experimentation into a 40-point checklist covering sleep timing, sun exposure, avoiding added sugar, lifting heavy, oral hygiene, hearing protection, and pre-noon caffeine.

title: Bryan Johnson's peptide list updated 2026
description: Current Blueprint peptide protocol: topical 8-peptide hair serum daily, 20-30g oral collagen + vitamin C, Tirzepatide + CJC-1295 DAC experimental stack; Cerebrolysin, Epithalon, Thymulin all dropped.

title: Rapamycin in endometriosis and fertility: a new frontier
description: Clinical synthesis: Columbia VIBRANT and Li et al. Cell Reports Medicine RCT show 1 mg/day rapamycin for 21-28 days raised IVF clinical pregnancy rate from 28% to 50% in poor responders.
